source.title: CRC landmark

finding.description: Landmark CRC ctDNA MRD evidence published in NEJM 2022
finding.effect_summary: Landmark CRC evidence

source_excerpt: "A ctDNA-guided approach to the treatment of stage II colon cancer reduced adjuvant chemotherapy use without compromising recurrence-free survival."

finding.description: Bladder cancer ctDNA evidence published in NEJM 2024
finding.effect_summary: Bladder cancer evidence

source.title: NCCN Colon Cancer Guidelines v1.2025

finding.description: NCCN includes ctDNA MRD recommendations for CRC Category 2A, stage II
finding.effect_summary: Category 2A recommendation

source.title: ASCO PCO 2024 — CRC ctDNA

finding.description: ASCO PCO covers CRC stage II-III ctDNA MRD testing
finding.effect_summary: Stage II-III coverage

source.title: lead time

finding.description: Lead time from ctDNA detection to radiographic recurrence ranges from 3-12 months
finding.effect_summary: 3-12 month lead time

source_excerpt: Serial ctDNA analyses revealed disease recurrence up to 16.5 months ahead of standard-of-care radiologic imaging (mean, 8.7 months; range, 0.8-16.5 months).

source.title: COSMOS specificity

finding.description: Guardant Reveal COSMOS specificity 98.2%
finding.effect_summary: 98.2% specificity

source.title: COSMOS lead time

finding.description: Guardant Reveal COSMOS median lead time 5.3 months
finding.effect_summary: 5.3 month median lead time

source.title: CMS MolDX LCD L39256

finding.description: Medicare covers tumor-informed ctDNA MRD across solid tumors via MolDX LCD L39256
finding.effect_summary: Medicare coverage for solid tumors

source.title: OpenOnco Expert Advisory Panel

finding.description: Clinical sensitivity (percentage of recurred patients correctly identified as MRD-positive) differs from analytical sensitivity (detection rate in lab validation conditions) and should be distinguished when interpreting MRD test performance.

source.title: OpenOnco Expert Advisory Panel

finding.description: Landmark sensitivity (detection at a single post-surgery timepoint) typically yields lower numbers than longitudinal sensitivity (detection at any timepoint across multiple draws) and should be distinguished when comparing MRD tests.

source.title: OpenOnco Expert Advisory Panel

finding.description: Stage II cancers present the greatest MRD detection challenge due to lower tumor burden and less circulating tumor DNA, yet represent where MRD-guided therapy decisions often have the most impact.

source.title: OpenOnco Expert Advisory Panel

finding.description: Analytical specificity (correct identification of negative samples in lab) differs from clinical specificity (MRD-negative results corresponding to no recurrence) and both metrics answer different clinical questions.

source.title: OpenOnco Expert Advisory Panel

finding.description: With serial MRD testing, even 99% analytical specificity results in approximately 5% cumulative false positive probability over 5 annual tests, making analytical specificity particularly important for repeat monitoring scenarios.

source.title: OpenOnco Expert Advisory Panel

finding.description: LOD (limit of detection) values cannot be compared directly across different MRD test architectures due to different units, pre-analytical factors, and methodological differences.

source.title: OpenOnco Expert Advisory Panel

finding.description: When LOD is significantly lower than LOD95, MRD tests may offer additional surveillance potential through serial testing, as multiple samples provide repeated detection opportunities below the LOD95 threshold.

source.title: OpenOnco Expert Advisory Panel

finding.description: The amount of cfDNA input to the MRD assay (measured in ng) can be as relevant as blood volume (mL) for test performance, as different extraction methods yield different cfDNA amounts from the same blood volume.

source.title: OpenOnco Expert Advisory Panel

finding.description: Clinical sensitivity from interventional MRD trials requires careful interpretation because treatment effects on MRD-positive patients make it difficult to determine how many would have recurred without intervention.

source.title: OpenOnco Expert Advisory Panel

finding.description: MRD tests support three distinct clinical use cases (landmark post-surgery decision-making, treatment response monitoring, and surveillance) with different performance requirements and sensitivity figures that may not be interchangeable across use cases.

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA-based detection of molecular residual disease in isocitrate dehydrogenase-mutant cholangiocarcinoma for biomarker-guided therapy.

finding.description: ctDNA dynamics including persistent postoperative or post-chemotherapy positivity or rising levels during treatment with IDH inhibitors are associated with poorer disease-free and overall survival in IDH-mutant cholangiocarcinoma
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA dynamics associated with worse survival outcomes

source.title: Postoperative MRD status Refines Recurrence Risk Stratification Beyond Pathologic Response After Neoadjuvant Chemoradiotherapy for ESCC.

finding.description: Study included 93 patients with locally advanced esophageal squamous cell carcinoma who underwent neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy followed by esophagectomy between 2019-2023
finding.effect_summary: 93 ESCC patients analyzed with 36.5 month median follow-up

source.title: Postoperative MRD status Refines Recurrence Risk Stratification Beyond Pathologic Response After Neoadjuvant Chemoradiotherapy for ESCC.

finding.description: 30 out of 93 patients experienced recurrences, with most recurrences (73.3%) occurring within the first year post-surgery
finding.effect_summary: 73.3% of recurrences occurred within first year

source.title: Postoperative MRD status Refines Recurrence Risk Stratification Beyond Pathologic Response After Neoadjuvant Chemoradiotherapy for ESCC.

finding.description: 1-year recurrence rate was lower in pCR patients (13.5%) compared to non-pCR patients (30.4%), but this difference was not statistically significant
finding.effect_summary: pCR vs non-pCR: 13.5% vs 30.4% 1-year recurrence (p=0.061)

source.title: Postoperative MRD status Refines Recurrence Risk Stratification Beyond Pathologic Response After Neoadjuvant Chemoradiotherapy for ESCC.

finding.description: MRD status significantly stratified recurrence-free survival and early recurrence risk, with 1-year recurrence rates of 6.1% vs 66.7% for MRD-negative vs MRD-positive groups
finding.effect_summary: MRD-negative vs MRD-positive: 6.1% vs 66.7% 1-year recurrence (p<0.001)

source.title: Postoperative MRD status Refines Recurrence Risk Stratification Beyond Pathologic Response After Neoadjuvant Chemoradiotherapy for ESCC.

finding.description: MRD positivity was an independent predictor of recurrence-free survival in multivariable Cox regression analysis
finding.effect_summary: MRD positivity: HR 13.62 for recurrence (p<0.001)

source.title: Postoperative MRD status Refines Recurrence Risk Stratification Beyond Pathologic Response After Neoadjuvant Chemoradiotherapy for ESCC.

finding.description: Combined pCR/non-pCR and MRD status classification identified four distinct recurrence risk subgroups with 1-year recurrence rates of 3.7% (pCR&MRD-negative), 7.7% (non-pCR&MRD-negative), 40% (pCR&MRD-positive), and 82.4% (non-pCR&MRD-positive)
finding.effect_summary: Four-group classification: 3.7% to 82.4% 1-year recurrence rates (p<0.001)

source.title: More Questions Than Answers in Trial of GRAIL's Galleri.

finding.description: The Galleri multi-cancer early detection test did not meet its primary endpoint of reducing late-stage cancers in the NHS-Galleri trial
finding.effect_summary: Galleri test failed to reduce late-stage cancers (primary endpoint not met)

source.title: Multimodal liquid biopsy surveillance after pancreatectomy for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma: evidence and a confirmation‑gated, trial‑ready framework.

finding.description: Tumor-informed ctDNA and longitudinal CTC kinetics show consistent prognostic value and may precede radiographic recurrence by weeks to months in resected PDAC
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA and CTC kinetics precede radiographic recurrence by weeks to months

source.title: Multimodal liquid biopsy surveillance after pancreatectomy for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma: evidence and a confirmation‑gated, trial‑ready framework.

finding.description: Post-operative minimal residual disease is often near the assay limit of detection, raising risks of false positives, over-imaging, and premature treatment escalation outside trials
finding.effect_summary: MRD near detection limit raises false positive risks

source.title: Multimodal liquid biopsy surveillance after pancreatectomy for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma: evidence and a confirmation‑gated, trial‑ready framework.

finding.description: EV markers and signatures provide complementary biology and may be informative in low-shedding states, but reproducibility is limited by pre-analytical and platform heterogeneity
finding.effect_summary: EV markers informative in low-shedding states but limited reproducibility

source.title: Liquid biopsy for biliary tract cancer: available evidence and future research directions.

finding.description: Evidence base for biliary tract cancer liquid biopsy assembled through targeted searches of PubMed/MEDLINE and Embase up to February 1, 2026

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA in human papilloma virus negative head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.

finding.description: Tumor-informed ctDNA assays achieve limits of detection near 0.01% VAF
finding.effect_summary: Tumor-informed assays achieve 0.01% VAF detection limit

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA in human papilloma virus negative head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.

finding.description: ctDNA is detectable in approximately 80% of HNSCC patients
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA detectable in ~80% of HNSCC patients

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA in human papilloma virus negative head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.

finding.description: In recurrent/metastatic HNSCC, baseline ctDNA detection exceeds 90%
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA detection >90% in recurrent/metastatic HNSCC

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA in human papilloma virus negative head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.

finding.description: ctDNA positivity consistently identifies patients at high risk for relapse, often months before radiologic detection
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA+ identifies high-risk relapse patients months before imaging

source.title: Circulating tumor DNA in human papilloma virus negative head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.

finding.description: On-treatment ctDNA decrease correlates with improved survival in recurrent/metastatic HNSCC
finding.effect_summary: On-treatment ctDNA decrease correlates with improved survival

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: Four DNA methylation biomarkers achieved perfect discrimination between PSC-associated CCA and PSC alone in tissue samples
finding.effect_summary: AUC=1.00 for PSC-CCA vs PSC discrimination in tissue

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: All four biomarkers achieved AUC >0.9 for distinguishing PSC-CCA from PSC alone in bile samples
finding.effect_summary: AUC >0.9 for all four biomarkers in bile

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: Two-biomarker panel (5-42952178 and PRKCB) in bile achieved 95% sensitivity and 90% specificity for PSC-CCA detection
finding.effect_summary: 95% sensitivity, 90% specificity for two-biomarker bile panel

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: Four-biomarker panel in bile (adding KCNA6 and ZFP82) increased specificity to 97% while maintaining 90% sensitivity
finding.effect_summary: 90% sensitivity, 97% specificity for four-biomarker bile panel

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: Two-biomarker bile panel detected all eight CCA cases (100% sensitivity) in samples collected 3-12 months prior to conventional diagnosis
finding.effect_summary: 100% early detection 3-12 months before conventional diagnosis

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: Two-biomarker plasma panel (KCNA6 and PRKCB) achieved 70% sensitivity and 90% specificity for PSC-CCA detection
finding.effect_summary: 70% sensitivity, 90% specificity for plasma biomarkers

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: Bile samples from 272 individuals and plasma samples from 128 individuals with PSC and/or CCA were analyzed across three different hospitals
finding.effect_summary: Multi-center study with 272 bile and 128 plasma samples

source.title: Validation of MyHPVscore: a high-performance HPV ctDNA lab developed test.

finding.description: MyHPVscore demonstrated limit of blank of 2.7 positive droplets/reaction for HPV16 detection
finding.effect_summary: HPV16 limit of blank: 2.7 positive droplets/reaction

source.title: Validation of MyHPVscore: a high-performance HPV ctDNA lab developed test.

finding.description: MyHPVscore demonstrated limit of blank of 2.6 positive droplets/reaction for other high-risk HPV types
finding.effect_summary: Other hrHPV types limit of blank: 2.6 positive droplets/reaction

source.title: Validation of MyHPVscore: a high-performance HPV ctDNA lab developed test.

finding.description: MyHPVscore demonstrated limit of detection of 15.2 positive droplets/reaction for HPV16
finding.effect_summary: HPV16 limit of detection: 15.2 positive droplets/reaction

source.title: Validation of MyHPVscore: a high-performance HPV ctDNA lab developed test.

finding.description: MyHPVscore demonstrated limit of detection of 20.8 positive droplets/reaction for other high-risk HPV types
finding.effect_summary: Other hrHPV types limit of detection: 20.8 positive droplets/reaction

source.title: Validation of MyHPVscore: a high-performance HPV ctDNA lab developed test.

finding.description: MyHPVscore demonstrated linear range of 15-62,500 targets/mL plasma for HPV16 with strong correlation (R² > 0.99)
finding.effect_summary: HPV16 linear range: 15-62,500 targets/mL (R² > 0.99)

source.title: Validation of MyHPVscore: a high-performance HPV ctDNA lab developed test.

finding.description: MyHPVscore demonstrated linear range of 12-100,000 targets/mL plasma for other high-risk HPV types with strong correlation (R² > 0.99)
finding.effect_summary: Other hrHPV types linear range: 12-100,000 targets/mL (R² > 0.99)

source.title: Validation of MyHPVscore: a high-performance HPV ctDNA lab developed test.

finding.description: Analytes were stable for 96 hours at -20°C and yielded consistent qualitative results after >400 days of storage
finding.effect_summary: Stable for 96 hours at -20°C and >400 days storage

source.title: Liquid Biopsy Biomarkers in Patients With Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer Receiving Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen Radioligand Therapy: Protocol for a Prospective, Longitudinal Multicenter Observational Study.

finding.description: [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 demonstrated overall survival benefit compared to standard of care in the VISION trial
finding.effect_summary: [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 prolonged overall survival (15.3 vs 11.3 months; HR 0.62)

source.title: Liquid Biopsy Biomarkers in Patients With Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer Receiving Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen Radioligand Therapy: Protocol for a Prospective, Longitudinal Multicenter Observational Study.

finding.description: [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 demonstrated radiographic progression-free survival benefit compared to standard of care in the VISION trial
finding.effect_summary: [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 prolonged radiographic PFS (8.7 vs 3.4 months; HR 0.40)

source.title: Liquid Biopsy Biomarkers in Patients With Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer Receiving Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen Radioligand Therapy: Protocol for a Prospective, Longitudinal Multicenter Observational Study.

finding.description: Only approximately 50% of patients achieved objective radiographic response in the VISION trial
finding.effect_summary: 50% objective radiographic response rate with [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617

source.title: Liquid Biopsy Biomarkers in Patients With Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer Receiving Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen Radioligand Therapy: Protocol for a Prospective, Longitudinal Multicenter Observational Study.

finding.description: LOOPS study planned sample size of 100 patients with primary endpoint evaluable in approximately 70 patients
finding.effect_summary: LOOPS study designed for 100 patients, 70 evaluable for primary endpoint

source.title: Liquid Biopsy Biomarkers in Patients With Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer Receiving Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen Radioligand Therapy: Protocol for a Prospective, Longitudinal Multicenter Observational Study.

finding.description: LOOPS study has 80% power to detect clinically meaningful differences in response rates between patients with and without ctDNA-based predictive markers
finding.effect_summary: 80% power to detect response rate differences with ctDNA markers (α=0.05)

source.title: Liquid Biopsy Biomarkers in Patients With Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer Receiving Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen Radioligand Therapy: Protocol for a Prospective, Longitudinal Multicenter Observational Study.

finding.description: LOOPS study recruitment status as of January 2026: 63 patients enrolled out of target 100
finding.effect_summary: LOOPS enrolled 63/100 patients by January 2026
